concepts: failed-auction reacceptance after stretch away from value
reset-then-go continuation with simplified trigger and asymmetric exits
expansion failure rotation with front-loaded monetization

next_cycle_focus: Keep only mechanisms that prove non-sparse participation on 1h lanes. If BTC reset continuation remains too thin, rotate away from continuation and lean harder into reacceptance and failed-expansion structures. If ETH failed-auction reacceptance and AXS expansion-failure rotation both trade credibly, refine management before changing the trigger grammar.
rationale: Cycle 64 remains an explore cycle inside an ETH/BTC/AXS 1h basket, but the first batch failed as sparse continuation logic. The live authority files still emphasize dense participation and basket-aware lane choice. This replacement batch rotates harder toward mechanisms that should produce more events at 1h resolution: ETH failed-auction reacceptance on the cleanest lane, BTC reset-then-go continuation with simpler logic on the best continuation lane, and AXS expansion-failure rotation as a management-first expression on the noisiest lane. The three specs remain materially distinct by mechanism, role assignment, and exit structure while staying strictly inside the supported/fetchable universe.
